title: Implement theme.ts-based ConsoleOutputRenderer replacing InkOutputRenderer (functional equivalence)
description: Build a non-Ink renderer (ConsoleOutputRenderer) that implements the existing OutputRenderer interface with full functional equivalence to InkOutputRenderer — all 9 render methods (renderStep, renderSuccess, renderError, renderWarning, renderInfo, renderSummaryCard, renderSection, renderComplete) plus async cleanup() — by composing theme.ts primitives (paint/symbol/SYMBOL_ASCII/ANSI/PALETTE/isColorEnabled) into output strings written to console, instead of mounting Ink/React components. Preserve the OutputRenderer interface (tui/types.ts) and a factory with the same signature semantics as createInkRenderer(config?). Wire install-v2.ts to use the new factory. Ink/react stay installed in this task so the tree compiles; deletion happens in TASK-002. Output behavior and wording must match the current Ink components — only the rendering engine changes.
action: Create packages/cli/src/tui/ConsoleOutputRenderer.ts: `export class ConsoleOutputRenderer implements OutputRenderer` + `export function createInstallRenderer(config?: OutputRendererConfig): OutputRenderer`. Implement every method on the OutputRenderer interface from tui/types.ts: renderStep(step: StepInfo), renderSuccess, renderError(err: ErrorInfo), renderWarning, renderInfo, renderSummaryCard(summary: SummaryInfo), renderSection, renderComplete, async cleanup(). Reuse the toErrorInfo helper logic from ErrorBox.tsx (inline it or move to a non-tsx helper). Render via theme.ts: import { PALETTE, paint, symbol, SYMBOL_ASCII, ANSI, isColorEnabled } from the shared theme module; resolve colorOn once via isColorEnabled(process.env, process.stdout.isTTY) honoring OutputRendererConfig.colors. EQUIVALENCE BAR: the target is content + wording + semantic color-role equivalence, NOT byte-identical glyphs/animation — the 6 live Ink components use a richer glyph/color set (○ ● ✓ ✗ ! ℹ 💡, rounded box borders, literal cyan/blue/gray/white) than theme.ts exposes (3 SYMBOL kinds ok/warn/error + 7 role tokens success/warn/error/drift/ai/human/accent, no cyan/gray), so map down to the theme vocabulary deterministically rather than reproducing Ink pixels. CONCRETE GLYPH MAP (step status): pending/skipped -> `○`, running -> `●`, success -> symbol('ok'), error -> symbol('error'), warn -> symbol('warn'), info -> `ℹ`. CONCRETE COLOR-TOKEN MAP: info -> 'ai', success -> 'success', error -> 'error', warn -> 'warn', running -> 'ai', muted/gray -> ANSI.dim (or 'muted'), accent/section title -> 'accent'. Map each Ink component to a string builder: SectionHeader->renderSection emits a bold accent ('accent') header line; StepCounter/Spinner->renderStep emits a static in-progress line (running glyph `●` + label) when running and a completed line (symbol('ok') + counter + label) when done; StatusMessage->renderInfo/Success/Warning emit the mapped glyph + paint(mapped-token, text); ErrorBox->renderError emits a MULTI-LINE PLAIN-TEXT block (error label + indented detail rows; box-drawing borders NOT required for W3-A equivalence) from ErrorInfo; SummaryCard->renderSummaryCard emits a MULTI-LINE PLAIN-TEXT block (label + indented detail rows) from SummaryInfo/SummaryDetailRow (box-drawing borders NOT required). Write output with console.log/process.stdout.write (match InkOutputRenderer's stream). cleanup() flushes/no-ops (no Ink unmount). Then in packages/cli/src/tui/index.ts add the export of ConsoleOutputRenderer + createInstallRenderer (keep Ink exports for now — removed in TASK-002). In packages/cli/src/commands/install-v2.ts line ~109-110 replace `createInkRenderer({verbose})` with `createInstallRenderer({verbose})` (import from ../tui), keeping shouldUseInstallRenderer gate, the InstallContext.renderer assignment (~line 112), and the `await renderer.cleanup()` call (~line 128) unchanged.

criteria: File packages/cli/src/tui/ConsoleOutputRenderer.ts exists and contains 'class ConsoleOutputRenderer' and 'implements OutputRenderer'

ConsoleOutputRenderer.ts contains a method or assignment for each of: renderStep, renderSuccess, renderError, renderWarning, renderInfo, renderSummaryCard, renderSection, renderComplete, cleanup (grep -c each name >= 1)
ConsoleOutputRenderer.ts imports from theme (grep 'paint' AND 'symbol' AND 'ANSI' present) and contains NO 'from "ink"', no 'from "react"', and no JSX

packages/cli/src/commands/install-v2.ts references createInstallRenderer and no longer references createInkRenderer (grep 'createInstallRenderer' present AND grep 'createInkRenderer' absent in install-v2.ts)

OutputRenderer SEAM invariant: the OutputRenderer interface body in packages/cli/src/tui/types.ts is byte-identical to HEAD. types.ts itself is NOT pinned with --quiet (TASK-002 deletes the dead InkOutputRenderer interface + react import there); verify via `git diff -- packages/cli/src/tui/types.ts` shows ONLY the react-import line + the dead InkOutputRenderer interface block removed when present — in TASK-001 no types.ts change is required yet, so its diff stays empty here

EQUIVALENCE BAR: ConsoleOutputRenderer reproduces content + wording + semantic color-role of the 6 live Ink components per the glyph/color map in `action` (NOT byte-identical glyphs/animation; box borders + live spinner animation are intentionally dropped, staying out of W3-B visual-redesign scope)
pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test -- install-v2-pipeline passes (renderer interface contract intact)
convergence.verification: cd packages/cli && grep -n 'class ConsoleOutputRenderer\|implements OutputRenderer\|createInstallRenderer' src/tui/ConsoleOutputRenderer.ts; grep -n 'createInstallRenderer\|createInkRenderer' src/commands/install-v2.ts; git diff --quiet -- src/install/pipeline/pipeline.ts && echo PIPELINE_UNCHANGED; git diff -- src/tui/types.ts && echo 'TYPES_DIFF_SHOWN (TASK-001 expects empty; OutputRenderer interface body must stay byte-identical)'; cd ../.. && pnpm -r exec tsc --noEmit && pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test -- install-v2-pipeline
convergence.definition_of_done: The install wizard renders section headers, step lines, status messages, error blocks, and summary cards with content + wording + semantic color-role equivalence to before (not byte-identical glyphs/animation) — produced by theme.ts string composition instead of Ink/React, behind the unchanged OutputRenderer interface, with install-v2.ts switched to the new factory and the type-check green.

files.change: Non-Ink OutputRenderer implementation: all 9 methods + async cleanup, composing theme.ts paint/symbol/SYMBOL_ASCII/ANSI/PALETTE strings to console, inlining toErrorInfo logic from ErrorBox.tsx. Static in-progress line for renderStep(running); completed counter line for renderStep(done).
Add `export { ConsoleOutputRenderer, createInstallRenderer } from './ConsoleOutputRenderer'`. Leave existing Ink exports in place (TASK-002 removes them).

Swap createInkRenderer({verbose}) -> createInstallRenderer({verbose}); update import from ../tui. Keep shouldUseInstallRenderer gate, InstallContext.renderer assignment, and await renderer.cleanup() unchanged.

implementation: Read InkOutputRenderer.ts to enumerate exact method signatures, the output stream used, config handling (OutputRendererConfig {colors?, verbose?, timestamps?, prefix?}), and what each method renders.
Read the 6 live tsx components (SectionHeader, StepCounter, Spinner, StatusMessage, ErrorBox+toErrorInfo, SummaryCard) to capture exact wording, glyphs, layout, and color token usage to preserve.
Read theme.ts to confirm available primitives (PALETTE 7 tokens + muted, paint, symbol, SYMBOL_ASCII {ok,warn,error}, ANSI {reset,bold,dim}, isColorEnabled). Compose from these; do NOT edit theme.ts.

Create ConsoleOutputRenderer.ts: constructor stores resolved config + colorOn = isColorEnabled(process.env, process.stdout.isTTY) gated by config.colors; implement each method as a string builder writing to the same stream InkOutputRenderer used.

Map components: SectionHeader->renderSection (bold accent header); StepCounter/Spinner->renderStep (static in-progress vs completed counter line); StatusMessage->renderInfo/renderSuccess/renderWarning (symbol+paint per token); ErrorBox->renderError (framed block from ErrorInfo via inlined toErrorInfo); SummaryCard->renderSummaryCard (detail-row table from SummaryInfo/SummaryDetailRow); renderComplete->final completion line; cleanup()->async no-op/flush.
Export ConsoleOutputRenderer + createInstallRenderer from tui/index.ts (keep Ink exports for now).
Edit install-v2.ts factory call site to createInstallRenderer({verbose}) and fix the import.
Run pnpm -r exec tsc --noEmit and pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test -- install-v2-pipeline; fix until both green.

success_metrics: install-v2-pipeline suite passes with renderer interface unchanged

reference.pattern: OutputRenderer interface as rendering-engine seam; theme.ts string-composition instead of Ink JSX

rationale.chosen_approach: Strangler-swap behind the stable OutputRenderer interface keeps pipeline.ts and the install-v2-pipeline mock contract untouched, so risk is confined to the new renderer; Ink stays installed during this task so every intermediate state compiles.
decision_factors: C-007 acceptance demands FUNCTIONAL EQUIVALENCE, not a redesign
pipeline.ts already depends only on the OutputRenderer interface — preserving it isolates the change
install-v2-pipeline.test.ts mocks @clack not Ink, so an interface-preserving swap keeps it green
rationale.tradeoffs: The equivalence bar is content + wording + semantic color-role, NOT byte-identical glyphs/animation. theme.ts exposes a narrower vocabulary (3 SYMBOL kinds + 7 role tokens, no cyan/gray) than the Ink components (○ ● ✓ ✗ ! ℹ 💡 + rounded box borders + literal cyan/blue/gray/white), so glyphs/colors are mapped down per the deterministic table in `action`: live spinner animation -> static in-progress line, box-drawing borders -> multi-line plain-text blocks. This keeps W3-A out of W3-B visual-redesign scope while giving the executor a deterministic target.

risks: If a needed glyph/token is missing from theme.ts, prefer composing from existing primitives. Editing theme.ts forces a parity-mirror (.cjs) update enforced by theme-parity.test.ts — flag and only do if strictly unavoidable.
Spinner->static line change is the one intentional behavioral simplification; verify no test asserts animation frames.
